dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (ya, sentence-final tag)
notes: Sentence-final 'ya' as a comprehension-check tag — speaker is softly confirming the listener follows. Similar but DIFFERENT from kan: 'kan' presupposes shared knowledge ('you know this already, right?'); 'ya' is checking comprehension after explaining something. English 'yeah?' captures the softer-check register; 'right?' is reserved for kan to keep the distinction. POLYSEMY NOTE: 'ya' is also a standalone 'yes' word — the regex requires preceding whitespace, so single-word 'Ya' won't match (no preceding char). 'Iya ya' (yes, right?) would match the sentence-final 'ya'. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'OK?' is also a candidate.
